target_journal.template_status: Elsevier article template selected; manuscript source remains in Markdown until author metadata and final figures are frozen
target_journal.required_word_page_limit: submit as single-column, double-spaced manuscript with numbered pages; Pattern Recognition guide indicates no less than 20 and no more than 35 pages for regular submissions
target_journal.supplement_format: separate supplementary material package with reproducibility tables, FAQ, limitations, and artifact manifest

funding_statement: <exact funding agency names, grant numbers, and recipient authors, or the journal-required no-funding wording>
conflict_of_interest_statement: <exact conflict-of-interest statement, or the journal-required no-conflict wording>
data_availability_statement: GitHub Release URL: <github release url>. Zenodo DOI: <zenodo doi>. Hugging Face URL: <hugging face model or dataset url>. The benchmark datasets used in this study are publicly available from their original providers subject to their licenses. Dataset download instructions are provided in DATASETS.md.
code_availability_statement: repository URL: <repository url>. The source code, experiment configurations, table regeneration scripts, environment files, Dockerfile, CI workflow, and SHA256 artifact manifest are available under <license>. The exact release commit is <commit sha>.
reproducibility_statement: All retained quantitative claims are tied to fixed configs, seeds, threshold policies, prediction arrays, and table-generation scripts. The selection policy uses normal images and synthetic anomalies only; real anomaly labels and masks are reserved for held-out audit. The release manifest reports SHA256 hashes for the reproducibility artifacts.
ethics_statement: This study evaluates industrial anomaly detection datasets and does not involve human participants, human tissue, personal data, or animal experiments.

cover_letter.manuscript_title: Lite-SEER-AD: Label-Free Feature-First Industrial Anomaly Localization with Selective Regional Verification
cover_letter.availability_sentence: All retained claims are backed by reproducible configs, fixed thresholds, prediction manifests, statistical tables, release metadata, and the final public artifact links.
